points: Number systems evolved through five key ideas: (1) one-to-one mapping and grouping (Gumulgal counts by 2s), (2) landmark numbers (Roman I, V, X, L, C, D, M), (3) powers of a base for landmarks (Egyptian base-10: 1, 10, 100, 1000,...), (4) place value notation where symbol position indicates the power (Mesopotamian, Mayan, Chinese), (5) zero as both a placeholder and a number in operations.

The Hindu/Indian number system (c. 2000 years ago) perfected all previous ideas: base-10 positional system using only 10 digits (0-9), treating zero with arithmetic properties, enabling efficient computation. It spread from India to Arab world (800 CE) to Europe (1100 CE) and is now used globally as the foundation of modern mathematics, science, and technology.
